cluster_label: I am learning to play with others
educational_objective: Support naming faces and feelings with short board and picture books.
age_stage_nuance: Just past two, feelings talk shifts from mirroring a face to naming a mood with a colour, photo or short line.
what_to_look_for: Clear faces or colour cues, short pages, age overlap 18-48m, UK publisher primary.
what_to_avoid: Interest age from 3+ only, abstract metaphor alone, Amazon-only primaries.
methodology: Bench of UK feelings and faces books on Penguin, Bookshop and Priddy; discarded interest-from-3 and 403/404 hosts; ranked by name-and-point fit for this band, brand cap two, and buyability; URL-verified 2026-07-24. age-gate filter against 25-27 months.
buying_factor_memo: Name-and-point first (colour, photo face, heart cue). Then short feeling lines and worry naming. Brand cap two per author/imprint. Specialist ratings path after Amazon bot walls.

top_picks.evidence_notes: UK publisher/Bookshop PDP smoke-OK and buyable 2026-07-24 (The Colour Monster). Amazon bot-walled in-agent; specialist exemption with long UK shelf presence.
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A colour sorted feelings story that gives each mood a clear jar and picture so you can name one feeling before you turn the page.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Just past two, the shift is naming a mood with a colour cue instead of only copying a face. Stay on one colour page, wait for a point or a matching face, then turn. Start here when you want a short feelings sort on the sofa.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #1: strongest colour-name pattern for this band; Ages 2+.

top_picks.evidence_notes: UK publisher/Bookshop PDP smoke-OK and buyable 2026-07-24 (In My Heart: A Book of Feelings). Amazon bot-walled in-agent; specialist exemption with long UK shelf presence.
top_picks.product_name: In My Heart: A Book of Feelings
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: Die cut heart pages that give each feeling a body image so you can stay on one heart and name it before you turn.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Just past two, feelings need a body cue, not only a face on the page. Stay on one heart, wait for a word or a point, then turn. Keep the pass short so the naming stays gentle after a hard goodbye.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #2: body cue feelings; Jo Witek first slot.

top_picks.evidence_notes: UK publisher/Bookshop PDP smoke-OK and buyable 2026-07-24 (The Feelings Book). Amazon bot-walled in-agent; specialist exemption with long UK shelf presence.
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: Bold pages that name everyday feelings in plain words, best when you pick one line and match it to today before you turn.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Just past two, short feeling lines land better than long stories. Read one line, match it to something from today, wait for a nod, then turn. Keep the naming pass short and kind on the sofa after a hard goodbye.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #3: plain feeling lines; Todd Parr first slot.

top_picks.evidence_notes: UK publisher/Bookshop PDP smoke-OK and buyable 2026-07-24 (Grumpy Frog). Amazon bot-walled in-agent; specialist exemption with long UK shelf presence.
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A funny frog story about feeling grumpy and finding friends again, best when you pause on the grumpy face and wait for a matching try.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Just past two, grumpy needs a face and a laugh, not a scold. Pause on the grumpy page, wait for a matching face or a word, then turn. Good when colour jars are already known and you want a story face.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #4: grumpy face play; Ed Vere brand distinct from Colour Monster.

top_picks.ownership_note: Skip if Colour Monster is not yet on the shelf.
top_picks.evidence_notes: UK publisher/Bookshop PDP smoke-OK and buyable 2026-07-24 (The Colour Monster Goes to School). Amazon bot-walled in-agent; specialist exemption with long UK shelf presence.
top_picks.best_for_tag: Best for colour feelings at school
top_picks.product_name: The Colour Monster Goes to School
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A follow on colour feelings story set around school nerves, still using clear colour cues you can name one page at a time.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Once Colour Monster is familiar, school nerves need the same colour naming job. Stay on one colour page, wait for a point, then turn. Second Anna Llenas slot only when the first Colour Monster is already earning sofa time at home.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #5: second Llenas; school nerves extension of colour naming.

top_picks.evidence_notes: UK publisher/Bookshop PDP smoke-OK and buyable 2026-07-24 (The Huge Bag of Worries). Amazon bot-walled in-agent; specialist exemption with long UK shelf presence.
top_picks.age_mark_on_listing: Picture book; toddler to early years overlap
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A story about a bag of worries that gets lighter when worries are named and shared, best when you pause as the bag changes.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Just past two, worry needs a name and a share, not a long talk. Pause when the bag changes size, wait for a word or a nod, then turn so the talk stays short and kind before bed tonight at home.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #6: worry naming story; Virginia Ironside first slot.

top_picks.evidence_notes: UK publisher/Bookshop PDP smoke-OK and buyable 2026-07-24 (Happy). Amazon bot-walled in-agent; specialist exemption with long UK shelf presence.
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A short board book about what makes a little owl happy, ending in a close cuddle you can name and copy.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Some evenings only need one happy word and a cuddle, not a full feelings sort. Read the short line, name the happy cue, wait for a lean in, then close. Soft close when bigger feelings books can wait until morning.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #7: short happy cuddle board; Emma Dodd first slot.

top_picks.evidence_notes: UK publisher/Bookshop PDP smoke-OK and buyable 2026-07-24 (Baby Faces). Amazon bot-walled in-agent; specialist exemption with long UK shelf presence.
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: Photo faces of babies showing clear expressions, best when you match one photo to a face in the room before you turn.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Just past two, photo faces still carry the naming job on tired evenings. Match one photo to someone in the room, wait for a try, then turn. Keep this when colour jars ask for too much talk after a long day.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #8: photo face match; DK first slot.

top_picks.evidence_notes: UK publisher/Bookshop PDP smoke-OK and buyable 2026-07-24 (Baby Touch: Faces). Amazon bot-walled in-agent; specialist exemption with long UK shelf presence.
top_picks.age_mark_on_listing: Board book for babies and toddlers
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A sturdy Ladybird board book of large black and white faces, sized for shared looking and simple pointing on the sofa.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Faces are still the social magnet just past two when colour books get busy. High contrast keeps the look simple while you name eyes and smiles. Start here when you want a short face book before busier emotion pages later.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #9: high contrast face looking; Ladybird first slot.

top_picks.ownership_note: Cap Ladybird at two with Baby Touch Faces.
top_picks.evidence_notes: UK publisher/Bookshop PDP smoke-OK and buyable 2026-07-24 (Baby Touch First Focus: Faces). Amazon bot-walled in-agent; specialist exemption with long UK shelf presence.
top_picks.age_mark_on_listing: Board book for babies and toddlers
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A first focus faces board book with a mirror ending so they can copy one expression after looking at the pages.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Just past two, the job is matching a face on the page to a face in the room. Copy one expression in the mirror, wait for a try, then close. Second Ladybird only when Baby Touch Faces already covers high contrast looking.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #10: mirror face ending; second Ladybird slot.

skips.product_name: Interest age from 3 years feelings title
skips.skip_reason: Interest age from 3 years does not overlap 25-27 months.
Amazon primary bot-walled in-agent; use publisher or Bookshop.
Waterstones 403 to agents; use publisher or Bookshop primaries.
DK.com PDP 404 in-agent; Baby Faces used via Bookshop instead.
Bloomsbury 403 to agents; Huge Bag of Worries used as worry alternative.

guidance_notes.title: Borrow first
guidance_notes.body: Colour Monster and In My Heart turn up in many UK homes; ask before buying new.

ingestion_ready.notes: Repaired Best for prefix + methodology age step; re-run FF.
